name: John Bunyan
title: Author & Preacher
bio: English writer and Puritan preacher best known for 'The Pilgrim's Progress,' one of the most significant works of English literature and the most widely read Christian allegory ever written.
faith: A Baptist preacher who was imprisoned for 12 years for preaching without a license. During his imprisonment he wrote 'The Pilgrim's Progress,' an allegory of the Christian journey from sin to salvation that has never been out of print.

sources.title: John Bunyan — Wikipedia
sources.note: Biography of the imprisoned preacher who wrote Pilgrim's Progress

sources.title: The Pilgrim's Progress — John Bunyan
sources.note: The most famous Christian allegory ever written, composed while Bunyan was imprisoned for preaching without a license — second only to the Bible in copies printed

sources.title: Grace Abounding to the Chief of Sinners — John Bunyan
sources.note: Bunyan's spiritual autobiography describing his conversion and inner struggles — a classic of Puritan devotional literature

tagline: Wrote the greatest Christian allegory from a prison cell
